senses.definition: a substance added to food to improve its taste, texture, or appearance

examples.x: Caramel coloring is a food additive used to give color to soft drinks and other foods.
Carrageenan is a food additive derived from seaweed that is used as a thickener and stabilizer in dairy products and other foods.
Salt is a common food additive used to enhance flavor.

senses.definition: a thin, translucent sheet made from rice flour and water, used in various Asian cuisines for wrapping spring rolls

examples.x: As the rice paper softened, I carefully layered sliced mangoes and coconut flakes, creating a sweet and tropical dessert roll.
He dipped the rice paper in warm water to soften it before filling it with shrimp and herbs.

senses.definition: a substance that is added to food, cosmetics, or other products to prevent or slow down their spoilage or deterioration

examples.x: Citric acid is a natural preservative found in citrus fruits and used in many foods and beverages to prevent spoilage.
The bread contains a natural preservative to keep it fresh longer.
Many processed foods have preservatives to extend their shelf life.
She prefers skincare products without synthetic preservatives to avoid potential skin irritations.

senses.definition: ink that is safe for consumption and used for printing on food items, such as cakes, cookies, and candies, for decorative purposes

examples.x: Chefs and bakers may use edible ink pens to add personalized messages or artwork to wedding cakes.
Edible ink is a popular choice for creating custom designs on cookies, such as monograms, logos, and holiday themes.

senses.definition: a substance added to food to change or enhance its color

examples.x: Blue food coloring is often used to give a bright blue hue to sports drinks and other beverages.
Natural food coloring made from beet juice or turmeric is used in some products as a substitute for synthetic food dyes.

senses.definition: a thin sheet of gold used for decoration or gilding

examples.x: Gold leaf can be added to edible flowers and other botanicals for a dazzling effect on salads or cheese boards.
Some high-end restaurants use gold leaf as a garnish for desserts and other dishes.

senses.definition: flavoring agents that are chemically synthesized and used to mimic natural flavors in food and beverages
an ingredient or substance used to increase the viscosity or thickness of a liquid or sauce in food preparation, such as cornstarch, flour, or gelatin

examples.x: The dessert mousse employed a gelatin-based thickening agent to create a light and airy texture.
The soup contained a thickening agent that gave it a rich and creamy consistency.

senses.definition: a substance used to add sweetness to food or beverages

examples.x: I prefer using honey as a natural sweetener in my morning oatmeal.

senses.definition: a substance that helps maintain the consistency, texture, and structure of a product

examples.x: My grandmother always uses a stabilizer when baking her famous cheesecake to ensure it maintains its perfect texture.
The frozen fruit bars used a stabilizer to prevent melting too quickly and maintain their shape.

senses.definition: an ingredient used to create gels, such as agar agar, pectin, or gelatin

examples.x: I found out that the fruit compote used a gelling agent to bind the fruits together.
The vegan gummy candies used a plant-based gelling agent to achieve a chewy and gel-like consistency.

senses.definition: a gelatinous substance derived from seaweed and used as a gelling agent in various food

examples.x: The agar cubes added a delightful chewiness to the tropical fruit salad.
The agar jelly shots were a hit at the party, with their vibrant colors and jiggly texture.

senses.definition: a natural substance found in fruits that is used as a thickening agent in food preparation

examples.x: I bought pectin at the store to make my own jelly from fresh fruits.
She added pectin to the strawberry jam to help it thicken.

senses.definition: a substance that helps retain moisture and prevents drying in various products

examples.x: The hair conditioner included a humectant that helped retain moisture and reduce frizz.
The tobacco products included a humectant to maintain the moisture content and freshness.

senses.definition: a substance used to provide a glossy or shiny appearance to food, such as beeswax, shellac, or vegetable oil

examples.x: The bakery used a glazing agent to give their pastries a delightful shine and preserve their freshness.
The donuts had a shiny appearance due to the glazing agent that coated their surface.

senses.definition: a substance used to improve the processing, handling, or performance characteristics of flour, such as ascorbic acid, enzymes, or malted barley flour

examples.x: The bread recipe called for a flour treatment agent to enhance the dough's elasticity.
The cake mix included a flour treatment agent to ensure a light and fluffy texture when baked.

senses.definition: something that is added to food to give it a better or more flavor

examples.x: The marinara sauce used a flavor enhancer made from sun-dried tomatoes, enhancing the robust and tangy taste.
The snack chips used a flavor enhancer that heightened the bold and tangy taste of the seasoning.

senses.definition: a substance or combination of substances used to enhance or impart a specific taste to food or beverages

examples.x: The cinnamon flavoring added a warm and aromatic touch to the baked apples.

senses.definition: a substance that helps mix and stabilize immiscible liquids, such as oil and water, to create a uniform and stable emulsion

examples.x: The hair styling cream included an emulsifier to combine the water and styling agents.
The salad dressing contained an emulsifier that kept the oil and vinegar blended together, preventing separation.

senses.definition: a substance used to maintain the color of food products

examples.x: The food packaging film had a color retention agent to prevent discoloration and maintain the attractive appearance of it.
The frozen berries included a color retention agent that prevented the vibrant red color from fading during storage.

senses.definition: a substance used in food production to add essential vitamins, minerals, or other nutrients to improve the nutritional content of the food product

examples.x: The fortified energy bar contained a fortifying agent that provided an additional dose of protein and fiber for sustained energy.
The fortifying agent increases product's nutritional value by adding essential vitamins and minerals to it.

senses.definition: a substance added to food to increase its volume or bulk, often used as a filler or to improve the texture and mouthfeel of the food product

examples.x: The low-calorie salad dressing used a bulking agent to create a satisfying texture without adding excess calories.
The meal replacement shake contained a bulking agent to create a thick and substantial drink.

senses.definition: a substance, such as vitamin E, that helps clean the body of harmful substances

examples.x: The blueberries were rich in antioxidants, which helped protect the body against free radicals.
The walnuts were a good source of antioxidants, making them a heart-healthy snack option.

senses.definition: a substance used to reduce or prevent foaming in food and beverage production processes

examples.x: The bubble bath included an antifoaming agent to minimize foam and maintain clear water.
The car wash detergent contained an antifoaming agent to reduce foam and ensure streak-free cleaning.

senses.definition: a substance added to food or beverages to prevent clumping or caking of powdered or granulated materials

examples.x: The confectioners' sugar had an anticaking agent that kept it light and fluffy.
The powdered sugar included an anticaking agent to prevent clumping and ensure a smooth texture.

senses.definition: a food additive that is used to adjust and control the acidity or pH level of food or beverages

examples.x: The salad dressing used an acidity regulator to achieve the perfect balance of tanginess and acidity.
They used an acidity regulator in the carbonated beverage to balance the tartness and create a refreshing taste.

senses.definition: a food additive that adds acidity to food or beverages, typically for flavor enhancement or preservation purposes

examples.x: The sour candy included an acidulant that added a zingy and mouth-puckering flavor.

senses.definition: a protein-based substance derived from collagen that forms a gel-like texture when dissolved in water

examples.x: The cheesecake filling included gelatin to help it set and hold its shape when chilled.
The mousse had a light and airy texture due to the incorporation of gelatin.

senses.definition: a meat product made from mechanically processed beef trimmings treated with antimicrobial agents

examples.x: Fast food chains removed pink slime from their menus to regain trust.
Public education campaigns highlight potential health risks of consuming pink slime products.

senses.definition: a natural, white substance, obtained from mines and also found in seawater that is added to the food to make it taste better or to preserve it

examples.x: Can you please pass the salt?
I added a dash of salt to the boiling water before cooking the pasta.
The chef sprinkled a pinch of salt to enhance the flavors of the soup.
The soup needed a little more salt to bring out the taste.
We bought a bag of coarse sea salt from the specialty store.

senses.definition: a chemical compound commonly used in baking as a leavening agent to help dough and batters rise

examples.x: Baking soda served as a natural toothpaste alternative, gently whitening teeth and freshening breath.
Baking soda sprinkled on a damp cloth effectively removed coffee and tea stains from cups and mugs.

senses.definition: a white powder that is used in baking products in order to make them rise and light

examples.x: Baking powder helped the biscuits achieve a perfect rise and a tender crumb.
The fluffy pancakes owed their light texture to the addition of baking powder.
